frameworks.name: Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: observer combines optic-flow likelihood with prior to estimate heading direction

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: sensory encoding constrained by neural coding accuracy in area MSTd

core_question: Do contradictory heading-from-optic-flow estimation biases across studies reflect true perceptual differences or methodological artifacts of response range?
key_assumptions: heading percept is generated by Bayesian inference combining prior and likelihood
sensory encoding reflects neural coding accuracy in area MSTd
reported estimates are a linear mapping from internal percept to probe response
underlying percept is invariant across response-range conditions
manipulations: size of the probe response range on the circle (80, 160, 240 deg)
heading direction sampled uniformly from +/-33 deg
key_findings: heading estimates scaled proportionally with response-range size
overestimation in large-range condition, underestimation in smallest-range condition
Bayesian observer model with linear range scaling quantitatively predicted estimate means and variances
interpretations: actual heading percepts are identical across response-range conditions
discrepancies between prior studies are explained by a linear percept-to-probe mapping, not perceptual differences
